Bahia, 2026-07-13T00:02:45+00:00 1134744 https://remoteOK.com/remote-jobs/remote-auxiliar-administrativo-comercial-remoto-empresa-confidencial-1134744 https://remoteOK.com/assets/logo-square.png The Role Artefacts / Asteria ART Robotics Simulation Engineer<br><br>By artefacts In jobs<br><br>Artefacts is building a set of software tools for robotics software developers, facilitating Continuous Simulation in their workflow and tight integration with CI. We hope to help them take on the challenge of creating an advanced robotized world that will enable a more efficient and environmentally friendly society. We also believe that emerging AI techniques will dramatically change the way robotics software is built.<br><br>The Role<br><br>The role focuses on creating tools to help robotic engineers simulate and test their robots in rich and relevant virtual environments.<br><br>You will work closely with engineers working on robotics who will use the tools you are building.<br><br>This means<br><br><ul><li>Create parametric / procedural 3d environments</li><li>Integrate AI tools to generate simulations or assets from text, images, etc.</li><li>Customization / tweaking of simulation engine performance for simulation at scale</li><li>Implement custom sensor models in simulation<br><br></li></ul>If you are interested in the role<br><br>Please consider contacting us to apply! We expect a few things from you:<br><br><ul><li>Enthusiasm and interest toward building realistic 3D environments as well as robotics and AI.</li><li>You will have room for growth, and many challenges.</li><li>We are enthusiastic and interested in our own specialties!</li><li>Ready to join a startup.</li><li>It can be messy, and requires personal commitment.</li><li>We expect you to work with us, not for us. We would like to see what you have done already.</li><li>Any publicly visible work such as open source contributions, personal blogs, Medium contributions, published paper etc.</li><li>Any publicly visible accounts such as Github, StackExchange etc.</li><li>Presentations at meetups / conferences?</li><li>Work / Projects you are particularly proud of<br><br></li></ul>In terms of technologies and skills, we are interested in experience with:<br><br><ul><li>Rust, Python or C++ or development experience</li><li>Experience using or building 3D Engines (Unreal, Unity, Ogre3D â¦) and physics simulation (Dart, ODE, PhysX)</li><li>Experience with parametric 3D modeling (Blender nodes, OpenSCADâ¦)</li><li>Experience with generative AI for 3D/CAD<br><br></li></ul>Below are optional<br><br><ul><li>Experience with robotics or robotics simulators</li><li>Experience with Photogrammetry / Nerf</li><li>Experience with cloud computing / high performance computing</li><li>Experience with GIS data sources</li><li>Experience with generative AI building or training ML models</li><li>Experience with 3D printing</li><li>Experience in UI/UX design<br><br></li></ul>The ideal candidate<br><br>If You Score Yourself High On All The Items, We Definitely Hope For a Chance To Talk With You. We Will Consider Some Extras<br><br><ul><li>Experience with âreal worldâ robot applications.</li><li>A real passion for Simulation Software or building Interactive Environments.</li><li>Graduate degree in related disciplines.</li><li>Degree is not necessary to apply, but if a work-visa is required, can make the visa-application process difficult without extensive, related work experience</li><li>The role would benefit from advanced analytical skills.<br><br></li></ul>Application stages: Getting to know each other<br><br>We hope applying to work with us is a great experience. We value your time and your interest! We think the best way to decide working together is first to know more of each other, starting with us sharing our online profiles.<br><br>We propose meeting a number of times, primarily online, and freely talk about you, about us, and about what we could achieve together.<br><br>As much as we would like to meet every applicant, we expect first reading about your motivation for applying, and seeing your past achievements. Smartphones these days have incredible cameras that are capable of taking smooth, sharp video, but the microphones are often lacking, to say the least. A wireless lavalier microphone can dramatically improve the audio quality of your videos, whether you’re the only one talking, or if you’re getting audio from multiple people. The DJI Mic 3 is one of the best options, and it’s currently discounted to $219 (usually $259) at several retailers, including Best Buy , B&H Photo , and Amazon . The bundle includes two microphones, a receiver unit that connects to your phone or camera, a charging case, power and audio cables, and four windscreens to reduce noise while recording outside. DJI Mic 3 Where to Buy: $259 $219 at Amazon (Two transmitter kit) $259 $219 at Best Buy (Two transmitter kit) $259 $219 at B&H Photo (Two transmitter kit) DJI made a number of improvements to the Mic 3 over previous generations to flesh out the feature set and improve compatibility. The receiver stores 32GB of audio, up from 8GB, with support for both 24-bit and 32-bit floating points, and has a built-in screen for checking battery levels and connection status. The included magnetic clips let you easily attach the transmitters to clothing or hats. There are also features that help these microphones work in less-than-ideal recording conditions. They offer two levels of noise reduction, and active gain control to reign in any unexpected loud noises. Each microphone can run for about eight hours on a single charge, with the receiver at a slightly longer ten hours.
